title: CompTIA A+ V15 Applied IT Support and Troubleshooting

audience.description: Beginning IT support learners who need a complete, safe, evidence-driven path from first support interaction through current CompTIA A+ V15 Core 1 and Core 2 coverage and independently defended troubleshooting practice.
entryKnowledge: Use a keyboard, pointer or equivalent input, browser, and local files well enough to complete accessible text-based simulations.
Read short technical instructions, compare observations, and perform basic arithmetic with units; the course teaches all required IT support concepts from first principles.
Agree to keep physical service, electrical work, accounts, networks, media sanitization, incident response, and production changes inside explicit authorization and training boundaries.
deviceConstraints: All browser labs analyze fictional deterministic support manifests. They never execute learner commands, access host devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment, alter configurations, erase media, contact users, or claim certification.
accessibilityAssumptions: Every ticket, diagram, command transcript, device procedure, simulator result, assessment, and project supports keyboard use, structured text, visible focus, reflow and zoom, non-color meaning, understandable status, alternatives, flexible timing, and user-chosen formats.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Use a keyboard, pointer or equivalent input, browser, and local files well enough to complete accessible text-based simulations.

A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Read short technical instructions, compare observations, and perform basic arithmetic with units; the course teaches all required IT support concepts from first principles.

A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Agree to keep physical service, electrical work, accounts, networks, media sanitization, incident response, and production changes inside explicit authorization and training boundaries.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 6 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 88 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: Every unique objective in current CompTIA A+ V15 Core 1 220-1201 and Core 2 220-1202, plus the official troubleshooting methodology competency standard taught before technical changes

Cumulative support practice across hardware, mobile, printers, networking, virtualization, cloud, Windows, macOS, Linux, ChromeOS, Android, applications, security, incident response, operations, communication, accessibility, privacy, sustainability, and performance-task defense

Two hundred observable competencies, six materially different stakeholder projects, misconception-targeted theory, guided workshops, faded debugging, changed-device labs, delayed retrieval, scenario quizzes, and one unfamiliar cumulative examination

Original deterministic support-evidence manifests that make authorization, safety, baseline, hypothesis, test, observation, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer limits mechanically reviewable
excludes: Real electrical or high-voltage repair, battery handling beyond trained policy, unauthorized device access, production network changes, live malware handling, forensic conclusions, legal or regulatory advice, destructive media action, credential collection, remote access, exam dumps, copied CompTIA questions, guaranteed certification, or work beyond learner training and authority

sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves user impact, asset and environment identity, authorization, safety, baseline, recent change, privacy, accessibility, least-destructive testing, evidence provenance, rollback, verification, documentation, escalation, and transfer boundaries before adding complexity.

Fictional browser cases never execute commands, inspect the host, connect to devices or networks, access accounts, change configuration, handle malware, sanitize media, open equipment, contact people, or imply authorization for real work.

Passing work requires a prediction, bounded discriminating test, observed result, changed case, rejected plausible failure, causal repair, side-effect check, prevention or recurrence plan, accessible user communication, and explicit remaining uncertainty.

Windows, Apple, Android, Linux distribution, firmware, wireless, USB, cloud, security, recycling, licensing, privacy, incident, and exam details can change; learners must name the version, date, jurisdiction, owner, and authoritative source for consequential transfer decisions.
Exam objective coverage sets minimum breadth, not maximum competence. Learners must connect each fact to a safe support decision and observable mastery evidence rather than memorize labels alone.

competencies.statement: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.
misconceptions: A support ticket automatically authorizes every diagnostic and repair action.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.

competencies.statement: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement, verify, prevent, and document as an evidence loop rather than a memorized script.
misconceptions: The troubleshooting method requires one rigid linear pass even when new evidence invalidates an earlier step.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement, verify, prevent, and document as an evidence loop rather than a memorized script.

competencies.statement: Record expected behavior, observed behavior, user impact, scope, frequency, severity, and a reproducible baseline without blaming the user.

competencies.statement: Rank causal hypotheses and choose one bounded, reversible, discriminating test with predicted outcomes.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rank causal hypotheses and choose one bounded, reversible, discriminating test with predicted outcomes.

competencies.statement: Verify original and changed cases, check side effects, preserve evidence, state remaining uncertainty, and write an accessible handoff.

modules.title: Support Evidence, Authorization, and Troubleshooting Method
objectives: Separate user goals, technician authority, system ownership, policy, safety, privacy, and escalation before touching state.
Apply identify, theorize, test, plan, implement, verify, prevent, and document as an evidence loop rather than a memorized script.
Record expected behavior, observed behavior, user impact, scope, frequency, severity, and a reproducible baseline without blaming the user.
